articles.headline: India Unveils National Circular Economy Policy 2024 to Boost Sustainability and Resource Efficiency
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government today launched the comprehensive National Circular Economy Policy 2024, aiming to integrate principles of reduce, reuse, and recycle across various sectors.
• The policy focuses on key sectors like electronics, construction & demolition, plastics, and automobiles, promoting sustainable production and consumption patterns.
• It introduces incentives for businesses adopting circular practices, R&D in green technologies, and frameworks for extended producer responsibility (EPR).
• The initiative seeks to minimize waste generation, enhance resource productivity, and create new green jobs, aligning with India's climate commitments.
• Special emphasis is placed on developing a robust informal sector integration strategy and digital platforms for waste exchange.
gsSubTopics: Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
Environmental impact assessment
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: National Circular Economy Policy 2024 launched by Union Government.
Key sectors targeted: electronics, construction, plastics, automobiles.
Aims to reduce waste, increase resource productivity, create green jobs.
Includes incentives for circular practices and EPR frameworks.
Focus on informal sector integration and digital waste exchange platforms.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of a circular economy for India's sustainable development goals and resource security.
Analyze the potential economic, social, and environmental benefits of the National Circular Economy Policy 2024.
Examine the challenges in implementing circular economy principles in India, particularly regarding informal sector integration and technological adoption.
Evaluate how this policy aligns with India's international climate commitments and targets.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration depicting a circular flow of resources with elements of recycling, reuse, and waste reduction.
articles.trivia: A true circular economy aims to eliminate waste and pollution, circulate products and materials, and regenerate nature, moving beyond the traditional 'take-make-dispose' linear model.

articles.headline: MEA Confirms 45 Indian Nationals Discharged from Russian Army, Efforts On for Further 50
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) announced that 45 Indian nationals who were serving in the Russian army have been successfully discharged.
• This follows continuous diplomatic efforts by India to secure the release of its citizens who had joined the Russian armed forces.
• The MEA stated that active negotiations are underway with Russian authorities for the release of an additional 50 Indian nationals.
• India has consistently advised its citizens against joining foreign military services, emphasizing the risks involved and the complexity of repatriation.
• The issue highlights challenges faced by Indian citizens seeking employment abroad and the government's role in protecting their welfare.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: 45 Indian nationals discharged from Russian army.
MEA leading diplomatic efforts for repatriation.
Efforts ongoing for release of 50 more Indians.
Government advisory against joining foreign military services.
mainsPoints: Analyze the complexities involved in the repatriation of Indian nationals from conflict zones, particularly when they are involved with foreign military forces.
Discuss the role of India's foreign policy and diplomatic channels in protecting the welfare of its diaspora abroad.
Examine the socio-economic factors that might compel Indian citizens to seek employment in high-risk environments and the government's responsibility in such cases.
articles.imageDescription: Indian flag alongside a map highlighting Russia, symbolizing diplomatic efforts.
articles.trivia: India has one of the largest diasporas in the world, with over 18 million people of Indian origin living abroad.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Validity of Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2024
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today upheld the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2024, dismissing petitions challenging certain provisions.
• The rules, framed under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, aim to govern the processing of digital personal data in India.
• Petitioners had raised concerns regarding potential infringements on privacy, surveillance powers, and the independence of the Data Protection Board.
• The Court emphasized the balance between individual privacy rights and legitimate state interests, affirming the robust framework for data protection.
• This decision paves the way for full implementation of India's comprehensive data protection regime.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Role of civil services in a democracy.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court upheld Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2024.
Rules framed under Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023.
Concerns raised included privacy infringement and Data Protection Board independence.
Court balanced privacy rights with state interests.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's decision on data privacy and digital governance in India.
Discuss the key features and objectives of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act and its associated rules, highlighting their significance for citizens and businesses.
Critically evaluate the challenges and criticisms leveled against the data protection framework and how the Court addressed them.
Examine the global best practices in data protection and how India's law compares to them.
articles.imageDescription: Gavel and Indian Constitution book, symbolizing legal judgment on data protection.
articles.trivia: The 'Right to Privacy' was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court of India in 2017 in the Puttaswamy case.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Polity
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy and Data Protection

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% Amidst Persistent Inflationary Pressures
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• The decision was taken unanimously, citing concerns over persistent retail inflation, particularly in food prices, and global economic uncertainties.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das reiterated the central bank's commitment to bringing inflation within the target band of 2-6% while supporting growth.
• The MPC also maintained its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance, indicating a continued focus on tightening liquidity.
• Analysts expect this pause to continue as the RBI monitors monsoon impact on food prices and global crude oil volatility.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy.
prelimsPoints: RBI kept repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
Decision by Monetary Policy Committee (MPC).
Reason: persistent retail inflation, especially food prices.
'Withdrawal of accommodation' stance maintained.
RBI Governor: Shaktikanta Das.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its implications for India's economic growth and inflation trajectory.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in managing inflation while supporting economic recovery in the current global and domestic scenario.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance in controlling liquidity and achieving monetary policy objectives.
Examine the impact of global factors like crude oil prices and geopolitical tensions on India's monetary policy decisions.
articles.imageDescription: RBI logo and a graph showing a stable interest rate line.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Test for Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today announced the successful completion of a crucial test for its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology demonstrator.
• The test involved a controlled landing experiment simulating the return of a spacecraft from orbit, validating key aerodynamic and guidance systems.
• This milestone brings India closer to developing cost-effective and sustainable access to space through reusable rocket technology.
• RLVs are designed to significantly reduce launch costs by allowing components, particularly the first stage, to be reused multiple times.
• The technology is vital for future space missions, including human spaceflight and commercial satellite launches.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: ISRO conducted successful test for Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology.
Test involved controlled landing experiment.
Aims for cost-effective and sustainable space access.
RLVs reduce launch costs by reusing rocket components.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's space program and its global standing.
Analyze the potential economic and technological benefits of developing RLVs, including their impact on commercial space ventures and national security.
Examine the challenges and complexities involved in perfecting RLV technology and how ISRO is addressing them.
Compare India's RLV efforts with those of other leading spacefaring nations.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle descending for a landing.
articles.trivia: The concept of reusable rockets was pioneered by SpaceX with its Falcon 9 and Falcon Heavy rockets, significantly reducing launch costs.

articles.headline: India-France Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Strengthening Defense, Space, and Maritime Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-France Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, focusing on bolstering cooperation in defense, space, cyber security, and maritime security.
• Discussions underscored the shared commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and addressing regional security challenges.
• Both nations emphasized joint development and co-production in defense manufacturing, building on existing agreements and technology transfers.
• Key areas of collaboration included counter-terrorism efforts, climate change initiatives, and multilateral cooperation at the UN and G20.
• The dialogue reaffirmed the strong strategic partnership between India and France, critical for regional stability and global order.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
India and its neighborhood- relations.
prelimsPoints: India-France Strategic Dialogue held in New Delhi.
Focus on defense, space, cyber, and maritime security.
Commitment to free and open Indo-Pacific.
Emphasis on joint defense development and co-production.
Collaboration on counter-terrorism and climate change.
mainsPoints: Analyze the pillars of the India-France strategic partnership and its evolution over the past decades.
Discuss the significance of India-France cooperation in ensuring stability and security in the Indo-Pacific region.
Evaluate the potential for enhanced defense and space collaboration between the two countries, including technology transfer and joint ventures.
Examine how this bilateral relationship contributes to India's broader foreign policy objectives and strategic autonomy.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and France crossed, symbolizing bilateral cooperation.
articles.trivia: France was one of the first countries to establish a strategic partnership with India after India's nuclear tests in 1998.

articles.headline: National Tiger Conservation Authority Introduces Advanced Methodology for Tiger Census
articles.explainer:
• The National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) today unveiled a new, advanced methodology for the upcoming tiger census, integrating cutting-edge technology.
• The revised approach incorporates artificial intelligence (AI) for image analysis, drone surveillance for inaccessible areas, and enhanced genetic sampling techniques.
• This aims to provide more accurate and real-time data on tiger populations, prey base, and habitat health across India's tiger reserves.
• The new methodology emphasizes 'M-STrIPES' (Monitoring System for Tigers – Intensive Protection and Ecological Status) protocols with greater digital integration.
• This move is crucial for strengthening conservation efforts and ensuring effective management strategies for India's national animal.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: NTCA introduced new tiger census methodology.
Incorporates AI for image analysis, drone surveillance, genetic sampling.
Aims for more accurate, real-time data.
Emphasizes M-STrIPES protocols with digital integration.
Crucial for strengthening tiger conservation efforts.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of tiger census methodologies in India and the significance of integrating advanced technologies like AI and drones.
Analyze how more accurate tiger population data can inform and strengthen India's wildlife conservation policies and strategies.
Examine the challenges associated with large-scale wildlife surveys in diverse geographical terrains and how technological advancements help overcome them.
Evaluate the success of Project Tiger and the role of NTCA in India's conservation achievements.
articles.imageDescription: A tiger in a forest with subtle digital overlay, representing technological census.
articles.trivia: India is home to over 70% of the world's wild tiger population, a testament to its conservation efforts.

articles.headline: Government Launches Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) 2.0 with Enhanced Features
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare today officially launched Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) 2.0, an upgraded version of the flagship digital health program.
• ABDM 2.0 introduces enhanced interoperability standards, a more robust health records management system, and improved patient data security features.
• The mission aims to create a seamless online platform for health services, connecting patients, doctors, and healthcare providers across the country.
• Key new features include AI-powered diagnostic support tools for doctors and personalized health recommendations for citizens based on their digital health records.
• This initiative is crucial for achieving universal health coverage and improving healthcare accessibility and efficiency in India.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: ABDM 2.0 launched by Ministry of Health and Family Welfare.
Features enhanced interoperability, robust health records management, improved data security.
Aims for seamless online health services platform.
New features: AI-powered diagnostics, personalized health recommendations.
Crucial for universal health coverage.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and features of Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) 2.0 and its potential to transform India's healthcare landscape.
Analyze the challenges in implementing a nationwide digital health infrastructure, particularly concerning data privacy, security, and digital literacy.
Evaluate how ABDM 2.0 can contribute to achieving universal health coverage and improving healthcare accessibility, especially in rural areas.
Examine the role of technology and AI in enhancing diagnostic capabilities and personalized healthcare delivery.
articles.imageDescription: Digital health interface with icons for doctor, patient, and health records.
articles.trivia: ABDM aims to create a digital health ecosystem similar to UPI for financial transactions, enabling seamless data exchange with consent.

articles.headline: India Actively Explores New Trade Routes via Chabahar Port to Boost Regional Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• India is intensifying efforts to operationalize and utilize the Chabahar Port in Iran as a critical gateway for new trade routes to Afghanistan, Central Asia, and beyond.
• Discussions are underway with Iran and other stakeholders to streamline logistics, reduce transit times, and enhance the port's capacity.
• The port offers a viable alternative to traditional routes, bypassing Pakistan and providing landlocked countries with sea access.
• This initiative is part of India's broader strategy to strengthen regional connectivity and foster economic partnerships under the International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC).
• The focus is on facilitating humanitarian aid and commercial goods movement, contributing to stability and prosperity in the region.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India exploring new trade routes via Chabahar Port in Iran.
Aims to connect to Afghanistan, Central Asia.
Bypasses Pakistan, provides sea access to landlocked countries.
Part of International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC).
Focus on humanitarian aid and commercial goods.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of Chabahar Port for India's regional connectivity initiatives and its geopolitical implications in the broader Indo-Pacific and Central Asian regions.
Discuss the economic benefits and challenges associated with developing and utilizing Chabahar Port as a trade gateway.
Examine how the Chabahar Port project fits into India's 'Act East' and 'Connect Central Asia' policies.
Evaluate the role of INSTC in enhancing trade and economic cooperation among member countries and its potential to counter other regional initiatives.
articles.imageDescription: Map showing Chabahar Port connecting India to Afghanistan and Central Asia.
articles.trivia: The Chabahar Port is located on the Sistan-Baluchestan province's coast in southeastern Iran, making it Iran's only oceanic port.

articles.headline: Government Conducts Comprehensive Review of 'One Nation, One Ration Card' Scheme Implementation
articles.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution conducted a high-level review of the 'One Nation, One Ration Card' (ONORC) scheme.
• The review assessed the scheme's penetration, challenges faced by migrant workers, and the efficiency of technology platforms.
• ONORC allows beneficiaries to claim their food entitlements from anywhere in the country, fostering food security for migrant populations.
• Discussions focused on improving grievance redressal mechanisms, ensuring biometric authentication success rates, and integrating remaining states/UTs.
• The scheme is a significant step towards ensuring the right to food and enhancing social welfare delivery across India.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes.
Issues relating to poverty and hunger.
articles.source: Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution
prelimsPoints: Review of 'One Nation, One Ration Card' (ONORC) scheme.
Conducted by Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution.
Aims to assess penetration, challenges for migrant workers.
Allows beneficiaries to claim food entitlements nationwide.
Focus on improving grievance redressal and biometric authentication.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and benefits of the 'One Nation, One Ration Card' scheme, particularly for migrant workers and vulnerable populations.
Analyze the challenges in the implementation of ONORC, including technological hurdles, inter-state coordination, and addressing beneficiaries' grievances.
Evaluate the impact of ONORC on ensuring food security and reducing hunger across India.
Examine how such portability initiatives strengthen the federal structure and social welfare delivery mechanisms.
articles.imageDescription: Ration card with a map of India in the background, symbolizing nationwide portability.
articles.trivia: The ONORC scheme is based on the technological integration of existing PDS systems in states/UTs with a central repository of ration card data.

articles.headline: India's Largest Integrated Solar Park Inaugurated in Rajasthan, Boosting Green Energy Capacity
articles.explainer:
• A new integrated solar park, touted as India's largest, was inaugurated in Rajasthan today, significantly boosting the nation's renewable energy capacity.
• The park, developed over thousands of acres, combines multiple solar power projects with advanced grid infrastructure and storage solutions.
• It is expected to generate gigawatts of clean electricity, contributing to India's target of 500 GW renewable energy capacity by 2030.
• The project highlights Rajasthan's potential as a solar energy hub due to its high solar insolation and vast barren lands.
• This initiative is a major step towards reducing carbon emissions and enhancing energy security, aligning with India's climate goals.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
New and renewable energy.
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: India's largest integrated solar park inaugurated in Rajasthan.
Combines multiple solar projects with grid infrastructure.
Expected to generate gigawatts of clean electricity.
Contributes to India's 500 GW renewable energy target by 2030.
Highlights Rajasthan's potential as a solar energy hub.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of large-scale solar parks in achieving India's ambitious renewable energy targets and ensuring energy security.
Analyze the economic and environmental benefits of investing in solar energy infrastructure, particularly in states like Rajasthan.
Examine the challenges in land acquisition, grid integration, and financing for mega renewable energy projects in India.
Evaluate India's progress towards its climate change commitments and the role of solar energy in this journey.
articles.imageDescription: Vast expanse of solar panels under a clear sky in a desert landscape.
articles.trivia: Bhadla Solar Park in Rajasthan was previously one of the world's largest fully commissioned solar photovoltaic power plants.

articles.headline: India Showcases Indigenous 5G Advanced Solutions at Global Telecom Forum
articles.explainer:
• India today showcased its indigenously developed 5G Advanced solutions and capabilities at a prominent global telecom forum.
• The demonstrations included advanced network slicing, ultra-low latency communication for critical applications, and enhanced IoT connectivity solutions.
• This highlights India's progress in becoming a hub for telecom technology innovation and a significant player in the global 5G ecosystem.
• The focus is on developing affordable, secure, and scalable 5G solutions tailored for both urban and rural deployment.
• This initiative supports the 'Digital India' vision and strengthens India's technological sovereignty in critical infrastructure.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: India showcased indigenous 5G Advanced solutions.
Demonstrations included network slicing, ultra-low latency, IoT connectivity.
Aims for affordable, secure, scalable 5G solutions.
Supports 'Digital India' and technological sovereignty.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of developing indigenous 5G Advanced technology for India's digital economy and national security.
Analyze the potential applications and benefits of 5G Advanced solutions across various sectors, including healthcare, manufacturing, and smart cities.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India in becoming a global leader in telecom technology innovation and export.
Evaluate the role of government policies and private sector investment in fostering a robust 5G ecosystem in India.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract digital network lines connecting various devices, symbolizing 5G connectivity.
articles.trivia: 5G Advanced, or 5.5G, is the next evolution of 5G, promising even faster speeds, lower latency, and enhanced capabilities for AI and IoT.

articles.headline: NCERT Releases New National Curriculum Framework for Secondary Education
articles.explainer:
• The National Council of Educational Research and Training (NCERT) today released the new National Curriculum Framework (NCF) for Secondary Education.
• The framework, developed in line with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, emphasizes multidisciplinary learning, critical thinking, and holistic development.
• Key changes include a focus on experiential learning, reduced rote memorization, and flexibility in subject choices for students.
• It also proposes revised assessment patterns, moving towards competency-based evaluation rather than mere content recall.
• The NCF aims to prepare students for the 21st-century challenges and foster innovation and creativity.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NCERT released new National Curriculum Framework (NCF) for Secondary Education.
Developed in line with National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
Emphasizes multidisciplinary learning, critical thinking, holistic development.
Proposes revised assessment patterns towards competency-based evaluation.
Aims to reduce rote memorization and offer flexible subject choices.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key reforms introduced in the new National Curriculum Framework (NCF) for Secondary Education and their alignment with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
Analyze the potential impact of these curriculum changes on student learning outcomes, skill development, and overall educational quality in India.
Examine the challenges in implementing such large-scale curriculum reforms across diverse educational boards and regions in India.
Evaluate how the NCF aims to prepare Indian students for the demands of the 21st-century global economy and foster innovation.
articles.imageDescription: Open book with diverse symbols of learning and knowledge, representing holistic education.
articles.trivia: The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century in India, replacing the 1986 policy.

articles.headline: India Reiterates Call for Urgent UN Security Council Reform at General Assembly Session
articles.explainer:
• India today strongly reiterated its call for urgent and comprehensive reform of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) during the ongoing UN General Assembly session.
• External Affairs Minister emphasized that the current structure of the UNSC does not reflect contemporary global realities, particularly the rise of developing nations.
• India advocated for an expansion of both permanent and non-permanent seats, seeking a permanent seat for itself and other deserving countries.
• The reform is seen as crucial for making the UNSC more representative, legitimate, and effective in addressing global peace and security challenges.
• India highlighted its consistent contributions to UN peacekeeping and its commitment to multilateralism, underscoring its credentials for a permanent role.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India reiterated call for UN Security Council (UNSC) reform at UN General Assembly.
Current UNSC structure doesn't reflect global realities.
Advocated for expansion of permanent and non-permanent seats.
India seeks permanent seat.
Reform aims for more representative, legitimate, effective UNSC.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's consistent demand for a permanent seat in the UN Security Council and the justifications it presents for such a role.
Analyze the arguments for and against the reform of the UN Security Council, considering the perspectives of various member states and geopolitical blocs.
Examine the implications of the current UNSC structure on its effectiveness in addressing contemporary global challenges like terrorism, climate change, and pandemics.
Evaluate India's role in multilateral forums and its contributions to global peace and security, reinforcing its case for enhanced global governance responsibilities.
articles.imageDescription: UN logo with a subtle Indian flag overlay, symbolizing India's push for reform.
articles.trivia: The UN Security Council has five permanent members (China, France, Russia, UK, US) and ten non-permanent members elected for two-year terms.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Uncovers Significant Ancient Artifacts in Maharashtra
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a trove of ancient artifacts during an excavation in the Vidarbha region of Maharashtra.
• The findings include pottery, tools, ancient coins, and remnants of habitations dating back to the early historic period (circa 6th-4th century BCE).
• This discovery provides crucial insights into the trade routes, cultural practices, and settlement patterns of ancient communities in the Deccan region.
• The site is believed to be a key urban center along ancient trade networks, connecting northern and southern India.
• Further analysis is expected to shed more light on the socio-economic and religious life of the period, contributing significantly to Indian history.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
History of India and Indian National Movement.
prelimsPoints: ASI discovered ancient artifacts in Vidarbha region, Maharashtra.
Findings include pottery, tools, coins, habitation remnants.
Dates back to early historic period (6th-4th century BCE).
Provides insights into ancient trade routes, cultural practices.
Site believed to be a key urban center.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing the history and culture of ancient India, with specific reference to the recent findings in Maharashtra.
Analyze how archaeological evidence helps in understanding the socio-economic life, trade networks, and cultural exchanges of early historic communities in the Deccan region.
Examine the role of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in preserving and promoting India's rich cultural heritage.
Evaluate the challenges and ethical considerations involved in archaeological excavations and the preservation of ancient sites.
articles.imageDescription: Excavated ancient pottery and tools laid out for examination.
articles.trivia: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) was founded in 1861 by Alexander Cunningham, who also became its first Director-General.

articles.headline: Government Boosts Border Management Initiatives with Focus on Technology and Community Engagement
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs today announced a series of enhanced border management initiatives to strengthen security along India's sensitive borders.
• The new measures emphasize the deployment of advanced surveillance technologies, including drones, sensors, and integrated command and control systems.
• Focus is also placed on improving border infrastructure, such as fencing, roads, and floodlighting, to deter infiltration and smuggling activities.
• Community engagement programs with border populations are being expanded to gather intelligence and foster a sense of security.
• These initiatives aim to create a multi-layered security grid, ensuring national security while promoting development in border areas.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: MHA announced enhanced border management initiatives.
Deployment of advanced surveillance technologies (drones, sensors).
Focus on improving border infrastructure (fencing, roads).
Expansion of community engagement programs with border populations.
Aims to create multi-layered security grid.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges faced in managing India's diverse land and maritime borders, highlighting the threats of infiltration, smuggling, and cross-border terrorism.
Analyze how the integration of advanced technology, such as drones and sensors, can enhance border surveillance and security capabilities.
Examine the importance of community engagement and welfare programs for border populations in strengthening overall border management strategy.
Evaluate the effectiveness of various border management initiatives in balancing national security imperatives with regional development goals.
articles.imageDescription: Border fence with surveillance camera, symbolizing enhanced security.
articles.trivia: India shares land borders with seven countries: Bangladesh, Bhutan, China, Myanmar, Nepal, and Pakistan, and Afghanistan (PoK).

articles.headline: New Report Highlights India's Progress in Reducing Fossil Fuel Subsidies, Calls for More Action
articles.explainer:
• A new global report on fossil fuel subsidies released today acknowledged India's significant progress in phasing out certain fossil fuel subsidies in recent years.
• The report, however, also highlighted that substantial subsidies for coal and oil still persist, hindering the transition to cleaner energy sources.
• It urged India to accelerate its efforts in redirecting these subsidies towards renewable energy and energy efficiency programs.
• Reducing fossil fuel subsidies is crucial for meeting climate targets, improving air quality, and freeing up fiscal space for developmental spending.
• The findings will inform discussions at upcoming international climate summits, where India's energy transition strategies are closely watched.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: Report on fossil fuel subsidies acknowledges India's progress.
Notes persistence of substantial subsidies for coal and oil.
Urges redirection of subsidies to renewable energy.
Reducing subsidies crucial for climate targets, air quality, fiscal space.
Findings relevant for international climate discussions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the economic and environmental implications of fossil fuel subsidies in India and the rationale behind phasing them out.
Analyze the challenges and political economy of reforming fossil fuel subsidies in India, considering their social and economic impacts on various stakeholders.
Evaluate how the redirection of fossil fuel subsidies towards renewable energy can accelerate India's energy transition and climate action goals.
Examine India's position on climate finance and equitable burden-sharing in the context of global efforts to combat climate change.
articles.imageDescription: A hand holding a sapling growing out of a burning fossil fuel, symbolizing transition.
articles.trivia: Globally, fossil fuel subsidies reached an all-time high of over $1 trillion in 2022, exacerbated by the energy crisis.

articles.headline: Government Notifies New Patent Amendment Rules to Streamline IP Process
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry today notified a new set of Patent Amendment Rules, aimed at streamlining the intellectual property (IP) application and examination process.
• The amendments introduce provisions for expedited examination for certain categories of applicants, including startups and small entities.
• They also simplify several procedural aspects, reduce compliance burdens, and incorporate digital processes for greater efficiency and transparency.
• The goal is to encourage innovation, protect intellectual property rights more effectively, and improve India's ranking in global innovation indices.
• This move is expected to boost domestic research and development and attract foreign investment in innovation-driven sectors.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: New Patent Amendment Rules notified by Ministry of Commerce and Industry.
Aims to streamline IP application and examination process.
Introduces expedited examination for startups, small entities.
Simplifies procedures, reduces compliance burden, incorporates digital processes.
Goal: encourage innovation, protect IP rights, improve global innovation ranking.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of robust intellectual property rights (IPR) protection for fostering innovation and economic growth in India.
Analyze how the new Patent Amendment Rules aim to streamline the patent process and benefit various stakeholders, particularly startups and small entities.
Examine the challenges India faces in strengthening its IPR regime and improving its global innovation ranking.
Evaluate the role of government policies in promoting research, development, and commercialization of indigenous technologies.
articles.imageDescription: Stylized image of a patent certificate with gears and innovation symbols.
articles.trivia: India's first Patent Act was enacted in 1856, providing exclusive privileges to inventors for 14 years.

articles.headline: G20 Health Ministers Adopt Declaration on Global Health Architecture, India Plays Key Role
articles.explainer:
• G20 Health Ministers, meeting virtually, adopted a significant declaration focusing on strengthening global health architecture and pandemic preparedness.
• India played a crucial role in drafting the declaration, advocating for equitable access to vaccines, diagnostics, and therapeutics globally.
• The declaration emphasized the need for enhanced surveillance systems, resilient health supply chains, and collaborative research and development.
• It called for greater coordination between international health organizations and financial institutions to ensure sustainable health financing.
• This collective commitment aims to prevent future health crises and build a more inclusive and robust global health system.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: G20 Health Ministers adopted declaration on global health architecture.
India played key role, advocating for equitable access to health tools.
Declaration emphasized surveillance, resilient supply chains, R&D.
Called for coordination between health and financial institutions.
Aims to prevent future health crises.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the G20 Health Ministers' declaration in strengthening global health architecture and pandemic preparedness, particularly from India's perspective.
Analyze India's role and contributions to global health initiatives, including its advocacy for equitable access to vaccines and medicines.
Examine the key lessons learned from recent pandemics and how they inform the current strategies for building resilient health systems.
Evaluate the challenges in achieving global health equity and the role of multilateral cooperation in overcoming them.
articles.imageDescription: G20 logo with medical symbols and a globe, representing global health cooperation.
articles.trivia: The G20, or Group of Twenty, is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union (EU). It works to address major issues related to the global economy.

articles.headline: Ministry of Environment Issues New Guidelines for E-Waste Management and Recycling
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change today released comprehensive new guidelines for the management and recycling of electronic waste (e-waste).
• The updated rules aim to enhance the collection, dismantling, and recycling infrastructure for e-waste, promoting a circular economy approach.
• They introduce stricter Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) targets for manufacturers and importers, along with penalties for non-compliance.
• The guidelines also focus on creating awareness among consumers about responsible disposal and formalizing the informal e-waste sector.
• This move is critical to mitigate environmental pollution from hazardous e-waste and recover valuable resources.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Environment issued new e-waste management guidelines.
Aims to enhance collection, dismantling, recycling infrastructure.
Stricter Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) targets.
Focus on consumer awareness and formalizing informal sector.
Critical for mitigating pollution and recovering resources.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing challenge of e-waste management in India and its environmental and health implications.
Analyze the key features of the new e-waste management guidelines, particularly the enhanced Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) framework.
Examine the role of formalizing the informal e-waste sector in achieving efficient and sustainable recycling practices.
Evaluate how these guidelines contribute to India's broader goals of circular economy and sustainable resource management.
articles.imageDescription: Pile of electronic waste with recycling symbols, representing the challenge and solution.
articles.trivia: India is one of the top five e-waste generating countries in the world, with a significant portion handled by the informal sector.

highlights: India launches National Circular Economy Policy 2024 to promote sustainability and resource efficiency across key sectors.
MEA confirms 45 Indian nationals discharged from Russian army; diplomatic efforts continue for further 50.
Supreme Court upholds Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2024, paving way for full implementation of data protection regime.
RBI maintains repo rate at 6.5% amidst persistent inflationary pressures, signaling continued 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
ISRO successfully conducts critical test for next-gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology, advancing cost-effective space access.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the National Circular Economy Policy 2024:
1. It focuses on sectors like electronics, construction & demolition, plastics, and automobiles.
2. It introduces incentives for businesses adopting circular practices and frameworks for Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR).
3. The policy aims to increase waste generation while enhancing resource productivity.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as the policy targets key sectors like electronics, construction & demolition, plastics, and automobiles. Statement 2 is correct as it introduces incentives for circular practices and EPR frameworks. Statement 3 is incorrect; the policy aims to *minimize* waste generation, not increase it, while enhancing resource productivity.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDPA), 2023 and its rules, consider the following statements:
1. The Supreme Court of India recently upheld the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2024.
2. The DPDPA aims to govern the processing of digital personal data in India.
3. The Act defines 'Data Fiduciary' as an individual who collects and processes personal data.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct, as the Supreme Court upheld the rules. Statement 2 is correct, as the DPDPA's primary aim is to govern digital personal data processing. Statement 3 is incorrect; a 'Data Fiduciary' is defined as any person who determines the purpose and means of processing personal data, which can be an individual, company, state, etc., not exclusively an individual who collects and processes data.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is the primary objective of the 'One Nation, One Ration Card' (ONORC) scheme?
options: (a) To provide free ration to all citizens across the country.
(b) To enable beneficiaries to claim their food entitlements from any Public Distribution System (PDS) shop in India.
(c) To standardize the quality of food grains distributed through PDS across all states.
(d) To replace all existing state-specific ration cards with a single national ration card.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) To enable beneficiaries to claim their food entitlements from any Public Distribution System (PDS) shop in India.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The primary objective of the ONORC scheme is to ensure food security for migrant populations by allowing beneficiaries to claim their subsidized food grains from any PDS shop in the country, irrespective of their original ration card's issuing state/UT. It does not necessarily provide free ration to all, standardize quality, or replace all physical cards with a single national card but rather enables portability.

prelimsMocks.question:
Regarding India's approach to UN Security Council (UNSC) reform, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. India advocates for an expansion of both permanent and non-permanent seats in the UNSC.
2. India seeks a permanent seat for itself in the reformed UNSC.
3. India believes the current UNSC structure accurately reflects contemporary global realities.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct, as India consistently advocates for expansion in both categories. Statement 2 is correct, as securing a permanent seat is a key foreign policy objective for India. Statement 3 is incorrect, as India argues that the current UNSC structure is outdated and does not reflect contemporary global realities, especially the rise of developing nations.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'M-STrIPES' (Monitoring System for Tigers – Intensive Protection and Ecological Status) protocols are primarily associated with which of the following initiatives?
options: (b) Tracking migratory bird populations in wetlands.
(c) Tiger conservation and census in India.
(d) Monitoring marine biodiversity in coastal areas.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Tiger conservation and census in India.
prelimsMocks.explanation: M-STrIPES is a software-based monitoring system launched by the National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) to facilitate intensive protection and ecological status of tiger reserves, playing a crucial role in tiger conservation and census efforts in India.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the potential economic, social, and environmental benefits of India's newly launched National Circular Economy Policy 2024. What challenges might arise in its implementation, and how can they be addressed?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Environmental pollution and degradation, Conservation; Indian Economy.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the concept of Circular Economy and the new policy. Discuss economic benefits (resource efficiency, job creation, new industries), social benefits (health, waste pickers integration), and environmental benefits (pollution reduction, climate action). Identify implementation challenges such as lack of infrastructure, informal sector integration, technological gaps, and behavioral change. Suggest solutions including policy incentives, R&D, capacity building, and public awareness campaigns.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's decision to uphold the Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2024, marks a significant step in India's digital governance. Discuss the key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDPA) and its rules, and evaluate their implications for individual privacy and the digital economy in India.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors; Indian Constitution (Fundamental Rights).
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by contextualizing the SC decision and the importance of data protection. Explain key provisions of DPDPA (e.g., Data Fiduciary, Data Principal rights, consent, Data Protection Board, cross-border data transfer rules). Analyze implications for individual privacy (empowerment, grievance redressal) and the digital economy (compliance burden, trust, innovation). Discuss the balance between state access to data and individual rights, and potential for a robust digital economy.

mainsMocks.question: India's consistent call for United Nations Security Council (UNSC) reform reflects its aspirations for a greater role in global governance. Examine the reasons behind India's demand for a permanent seat in the UNSC and discuss the challenges in achieving comprehensive reform of the organization.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate; Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce India's long-standing demand for UNSC reform. Outline reasons for India's claim (economic power, population, democratic values, contributions to UN peacekeeping, nuclear power status, regional influence). Discuss the challenges to reform (veto power of P5, differing views among member states, regional rivalries, 'Uniting for Consensus' group). Conclude with the importance of a reformed UNSC for global legitimacy and effectiveness.

quickBytes.text: The 'National Circular Economy Policy 2024' aims to achieve zero-waste and resource optimization.
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, replaced the Personal Data Protection Bill, 2019.
Repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks in India.
ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) program is a key step towards reducing space mission costs.
The Chabahar Port in Iran is strategically important for India to access Afghanistan and Central Asia.
Project Tiger was launched in 1973 to protect tigers in India's national parks.
Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) aims to create a national digital health ecosystem.
The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 promotes multidisciplinary and holistic education.
India is the world's third-largest producer of renewable energy, after China and the USA.
The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) is responsible for archaeological research and heritage preservation.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Uncovers Significant Ancient Artifacts in Maharashtra
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a trove of ancient artifacts during an excavation in the Vidarbha region of Maharashtra.
• The findings include pottery, tools, ancient coins, and remnants of habitations dating back to the early historic period (circa 6th-4th century BCE).
• This discovery provides crucial insights into the trade routes, cultural practices, and settlement patterns of ancient communities in the Deccan region.
• The site is believed to be a key urban center along ancient trade networks, connecting northern and southern India.
• Further analysis is expected to shed more light on the socio-economic and religious life of the period, contributing significantly to Indian history.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
History of India and Indian National Movement.
prelimsPoints: ASI discovered ancient artifacts in Vidarbha region, Maharashtra.
Findings include pottery, tools, coins, habitation remnants.
Dates back to early historic period (6th-4th century BCE).
Provides insights into ancient trade routes, cultural practices.
Site believed to be a key urban center.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing the history and culture of ancient India, with specific reference to the recent findings in Maharashtra.
Analyze how archaeological evidence helps in understanding the socio-economic life, trade networks, and cultural exchanges of early historic communities in the Deccan region.
Examine the role of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in preserving and promoting India's rich cultural heritage.
Evaluate the challenges and ethical considerations involved in archaeological excavations and the preservation of ancient sites.
GS1.imageDescription: Excavated ancient pottery and tools laid out for examination.
GS1.trivia: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) was founded in 1861 by Alexander Cunningham, who also became its first Director-General.

GS2.headline: MEA Confirms 45 Indian Nationals Discharged from Russian Army, Efforts On for Further 50
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) announced that 45 Indian nationals who were serving in the Russian army have been successfully discharged.
• This follows continuous diplomatic efforts by India to secure the release of its citizens who had joined the Russian armed forces.
• The MEA stated that active negotiations are underway with Russian authorities for the release of an additional 50 Indian nationals.
• India has consistently advised its citizens against joining foreign military services, emphasizing the risks involved and the complexity of repatriation.
• The issue highlights challenges faced by Indian citizens seeking employment abroad and the government's role in protecting their welfare.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora
prelimsPoints: 45 Indian nationals discharged from Russian army.
MEA leading diplomatic efforts for repatriation.
Efforts ongoing for release of 50 more Indians.
Government advisory against joining foreign military services.
mainsPoints: Analyze the complexities involved in the repatriation of Indian nationals from conflict zones, particularly when they are involved with foreign military forces.
Discuss the role of India's foreign policy and diplomatic channels in protecting the welfare of its diaspora abroad.
Examine the socio-economic factors that might compel Indian citizens to seek employment in high-risk environments and the government's responsibility in such cases.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian flag alongside a map highlighting Russia, symbolizing diplomatic efforts.
GS2.trivia: India has one of the largest diasporas in the world, with over 18 million people of Indian origin living abroad.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Validity of Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2024
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today upheld the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2024, dismissing petitions challenging certain provisions.
• The rules, framed under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, aim to govern the processing of digital personal data in India.
• Petitioners had raised concerns regarding potential infringements on privacy, surveillance powers, and the independence of the Data Protection Board.
• The Court emphasized the balance between individual privacy rights and legitimate state interests, affirming the robust framework for data protection.
• This decision paves the way for full implementation of India's comprehensive data protection regime.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Role of civil services in a democracy.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court upheld Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2024.
Rules framed under Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023.
Concerns raised included privacy infringement and Data Protection Board independence.
Court balanced privacy rights with state interests.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's decision on data privacy and digital governance in India.
Discuss the key features and objectives of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act and its associated rules, highlighting their significance for citizens and businesses.
Critically evaluate the challenges and criticisms leveled against the data protection framework and how the Court addressed them.
Examine the global best practices in data protection and how India's law compares to them.
GS2.imageDescription: Gavel and Indian Constitution book, symbolizing legal judgment on data protection.
GS2.trivia: The 'Right to Privacy' was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court of India in 2017 in the Puttaswamy case.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Polity
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy and Data Protection

GS2.headline: India-France Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Strengthening Defense, Space, and Maritime Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-France Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, focusing on bolstering cooperation in defense, space, cyber security, and maritime security.
• Discussions underscored the shared commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and addressing regional security challenges.
• Both nations emphasized joint development and co-production in defense manufacturing, building on existing agreements and technology transfers.
• Key areas of collaboration included counter-terrorism efforts, climate change initiatives, and multilateral cooperation at the UN and G20.
• The dialogue reaffirmed the strong strategic partnership between India and France, critical for regional stability and global order.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
India and its neighborhood- relations.
prelimsPoints: India-France Strategic Dialogue held in New Delhi.
Focus on defense, space, cyber, and maritime security.
Commitment to free and open Indo-Pacific.
Emphasis on joint defense development and co-production.
Collaboration on counter-terrorism and climate change.
mainsPoints: Analyze the pillars of the India-France strategic partnership and its evolution over the past decades.
Discuss the significance of India-France cooperation in ensuring stability and security in the Indo-Pacific region.
Evaluate the potential for enhanced defense and space collaboration between the two countries, including technology transfer and joint ventures.
Examine how this bilateral relationship contributes to India's broader foreign policy objectives and strategic autonomy.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and France crossed, symbolizing bilateral cooperation.
GS2.trivia: France was one of the first countries to establish a strategic partnership with India after India's nuclear tests in 1998.

GS2.headline: Government Launches Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) 2.0 with Enhanced Features
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare today officially launched Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) 2.0, an upgraded version of the flagship digital health program.
• ABDM 2.0 introduces enhanced interoperability standards, a more robust health records management system, and improved patient data security features.
• The mission aims to create a seamless online platform for health services, connecting patients, doctors, and healthcare providers across the country.
• Key new features include AI-powered diagnostic support tools for doctors and personalized health recommendations for citizens based on their digital health records.
• This initiative is crucial for achieving universal health coverage and improving healthcare accessibility and efficiency in India.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
GS2.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: ABDM 2.0 launched by Ministry of Health and Family Welfare.
Features enhanced interoperability, robust health records management, improved data security.
Aims for seamless online health services platform.
New features: AI-powered diagnostics, personalized health recommendations.
Crucial for universal health coverage.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and features of Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) 2.0 and its potential to transform India's healthcare landscape.
Analyze the challenges in implementing a nationwide digital health infrastructure, particularly concerning data privacy, security, and digital literacy.
Evaluate how ABDM 2.0 can contribute to achieving universal health coverage and improving healthcare accessibility, especially in rural areas.
Examine the role of technology and AI in enhancing diagnostic capabilities and personalized healthcare delivery.
GS2.imageDescription: Digital health interface with icons for doctor, patient, and health records.
GS2.trivia: ABDM aims to create a digital health ecosystem similar to UPI for financial transactions, enabling seamless data exchange with consent.

GS2.headline: India Actively Explores New Trade Routes via Chabahar Port to Boost Regional Connectivity
GS2.explainer:
• India is intensifying efforts to operationalize and utilize the Chabahar Port in Iran as a critical gateway for new trade routes to Afghanistan, Central Asia, and beyond.
• Discussions are underway with Iran and other stakeholders to streamline logistics, reduce transit times, and enhance the port's capacity.
• The port offers a viable alternative to traditional routes, bypassing Pakistan and providing landlocked countries with sea access.
• This initiative is part of India's broader strategy to strengthen regional connectivity and foster economic partnerships under the International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC).
• The focus is on facilitating humanitarian aid and commercial goods movement, contributing to stability and prosperity in the region.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India exploring new trade routes via Chabahar Port in Iran.
Aims to connect to Afghanistan, Central Asia.
Bypasses Pakistan, provides sea access to landlocked countries.
Part of International North-South Transport Corridor (INSTC).
Focus on humanitarian aid and commercial goods.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic significance of Chabahar Port for India's regional connectivity initiatives and its geopolitical implications in the broader Indo-Pacific and Central Asian regions.
Discuss the economic benefits and challenges associated with developing and utilizing Chabahar Port as a trade gateway.
Examine how the Chabahar Port project fits into India's 'Act East' and 'Connect Central Asia' policies.
Evaluate the role of INSTC in enhancing trade and economic cooperation among member countries and its potential to counter other regional initiatives.
GS2.imageDescription: Map showing Chabahar Port connecting India to Afghanistan and Central Asia.
GS2.trivia: The Chabahar Port is located on the Sistan-Baluchestan province's coast in southeastern Iran, making it Iran's only oceanic port.

GS2.headline: Government Conducts Comprehensive Review of 'One Nation, One Ration Card' Scheme Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution conducted a high-level review of the 'One Nation, One Ration Card' (ONORC) scheme.
• The review assessed the scheme's penetration, challenges faced by migrant workers, and the efficiency of technology platforms.
• ONORC allows beneficiaries to claim their food entitlements from anywhere in the country, fostering food security for migrant populations.
• Discussions focused on improving grievance redressal mechanisms, ensuring biometric authentication success rates, and integrating remaining states/UTs.
• The scheme is a significant step towards ensuring the right to food and enhancing social welfare delivery across India.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes.
Issues relating to poverty and hunger.
GS2.source: Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution
prelimsPoints: Review of 'One Nation, One Ration Card' (ONORC) scheme.
Conducted by Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution.
Aims to assess penetration, challenges for migrant workers.
Allows beneficiaries to claim food entitlements nationwide.
Focus on improving grievance redressal and biometric authentication.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and benefits of the 'One Nation, One Ration Card' scheme, particularly for migrant workers and vulnerable populations.
Analyze the challenges in the implementation of ONORC, including technological hurdles, inter-state coordination, and addressing beneficiaries' grievances.
Evaluate the impact of ONORC on ensuring food security and reducing hunger across India.
Examine how such portability initiatives strengthen the federal structure and social welfare delivery mechanisms.
GS2.imageDescription: Ration card with a map of India in the background, symbolizing nationwide portability.
GS2.trivia: The ONORC scheme is based on the technological integration of existing PDS systems in states/UTs with a central repository of ration card data.

GS2.headline: NCERT Releases New National Curriculum Framework for Secondary Education
GS2.explainer:
• The National Council of Educational Research and Training (NCERT) today released the new National Curriculum Framework (NCF) for Secondary Education.
• The framework, developed in line with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, emphasizes multidisciplinary learning, critical thinking, and holistic development.
• Key changes include a focus on experiential learning, reduced rote memorization, and flexibility in subject choices for students.
• It also proposes revised assessment patterns, moving towards competency-based evaluation rather than mere content recall.
• The NCF aims to prepare students for the 21st-century challenges and foster innovation and creativity.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NCERT released new National Curriculum Framework (NCF) for Secondary Education.
Developed in line with National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
Emphasizes multidisciplinary learning, critical thinking, holistic development.
Proposes revised assessment patterns towards competency-based evaluation.
Aims to reduce rote memorization and offer flexible subject choices.
mainsPoints: Discuss the key reforms introduced in the new National Curriculum Framework (NCF) for Secondary Education and their alignment with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
Analyze the potential impact of these curriculum changes on student learning outcomes, skill development, and overall educational quality in India.
Examine the challenges in implementing such large-scale curriculum reforms across diverse educational boards and regions in India.
Evaluate how the NCF aims to prepare Indian students for the demands of the 21st-century global economy and foster innovation.
GS2.imageDescription: Open book with diverse symbols of learning and knowledge, representing holistic education.
GS2.trivia: The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 is the first education policy of the 21st century in India, replacing the 1986 policy.

GS2.headline: India Reiterates Call for Urgent UN Security Council Reform at General Assembly Session
GS2.explainer:
• India today strongly reiterated its call for urgent and comprehensive reform of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) during the ongoing UN General Assembly session.
• External Affairs Minister emphasized that the current structure of the UNSC does not reflect contemporary global realities, particularly the rise of developing nations.
• India advocated for an expansion of both permanent and non-permanent seats, seeking a permanent seat for itself and other deserving countries.
• The reform is seen as crucial for making the UNSC more representative, legitimate, and effective in addressing global peace and security challenges.
• India highlighted its consistent contributions to UN peacekeeping and its commitment to multilateralism, underscoring its credentials for a permanent role.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India reiterated call for UN Security Council (UNSC) reform at UN General Assembly.
Current UNSC structure doesn't reflect global realities.
Advocated for expansion of permanent and non-permanent seats.
India seeks permanent seat.
Reform aims for more representative, legitimate, effective UNSC.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's consistent demand for a permanent seat in the UN Security Council and the justifications it presents for such a role.
Analyze the arguments for and against the reform of the UN Security Council, considering the perspectives of various member states and geopolitical blocs.
Examine the implications of the current UNSC structure on its effectiveness in addressing contemporary global challenges like terrorism, climate change, and pandemics.
Evaluate India's role in multilateral forums and its contributions to global peace and security, reinforcing its case for enhanced global governance responsibilities.
GS2.imageDescription: UN logo with a subtle Indian flag overlay, symbolizing India's push for reform.
GS2.trivia: The UN Security Council has five permanent members (China, France, Russia, UK, US) and ten non-permanent members elected for two-year terms.

GS2.headline: G20 Health Ministers Adopt Declaration on Global Health Architecture, India Plays Key Role
GS2.explainer:
• G20 Health Ministers, meeting virtually, adopted a significant declaration focusing on strengthening global health architecture and pandemic preparedness.
• India played a crucial role in drafting the declaration, advocating for equitable access to vaccines, diagnostics, and therapeutics globally.
• The declaration emphasized the need for enhanced surveillance systems, resilient health supply chains, and collaborative research and development.
• It called for greater coordination between international health organizations and financial institutions to ensure sustainable health financing.
• This collective commitment aims to prevent future health crises and build a more inclusive and robust global health system.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: G20 Health Ministers adopted declaration on global health architecture.
India played key role, advocating for equitable access to health tools.
Declaration emphasized surveillance, resilient supply chains, R&D.
Called for coordination between health and financial institutions.
Aims to prevent future health crises.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the G20 Health Ministers' declaration in strengthening global health architecture and pandemic preparedness, particularly from India's perspective.
Analyze India's role and contributions to global health initiatives, including its advocacy for equitable access to vaccines and medicines.
Examine the key lessons learned from recent pandemics and how they inform the current strategies for building resilient health systems.
Evaluate the challenges in achieving global health equity and the role of multilateral cooperation in overcoming them.
GS2.imageDescription: G20 logo with medical symbols and a globe, representing global health cooperation.
GS2.trivia: The G20, or Group of Twenty, is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union (EU). It works to address major issues related to the global economy.

GS3.headline: India Unveils National Circular Economy Policy 2024 to Boost Sustainability and Resource Efficiency
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government today launched the comprehensive National Circular Economy Policy 2024, aiming to integrate principles of reduce, reuse, and recycle across various sectors.
• The policy focuses on key sectors like electronics, construction & demolition, plastics, and automobiles, promoting sustainable production and consumption patterns.
• It introduces incentives for businesses adopting circular practices, R&D in green technologies, and frameworks for extended producer responsibility (EPR).
• The initiative seeks to minimize waste generation, enhance resource productivity, and create new green jobs, aligning with India's climate commitments.
• Special emphasis is placed on developing a robust informal sector integration strategy and digital platforms for waste exchange.
gsSubTopics: Environmental pollution and degradation
Conservation
Environmental impact assessment
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: National Circular Economy Policy 2024 launched by Union Government.
Key sectors targeted: electronics, construction, plastics, automobiles.
Aims to reduce waste, increase resource productivity, create green jobs.
Includes incentives for circular practices and EPR frameworks.
Focus on informal sector integration and digital waste exchange platforms.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of a circular economy for India's sustainable development goals and resource security.
Analyze the potential economic, social, and environmental benefits of the National Circular Economy Policy 2024.
Examine the challenges in implementing circular economy principles in India, particularly regarding informal sector integration and technological adoption.
Evaluate how this policy aligns with India's international climate commitments and targets.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration depicting a circular flow of resources with elements of recycling, reuse, and waste reduction.
GS3.trivia: A true circular economy aims to eliminate waste and pollution, circulate products and materials, and regenerate nature, moving beyond the traditional 'take-make-dispose' linear model.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% Amidst Persistent Inflationary Pressures
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) today decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• The decision was taken unanimously, citing concerns over persistent retail inflation, particularly in food prices, and global economic uncertainties.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das reiterated the central bank's commitment to bringing inflation within the target band of 2-6% while supporting growth.
• The MPC also maintained its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance, indicating a continued focus on tightening liquidity.
• Analysts expect this pause to continue as the RBI monitors monsoon impact on food prices and global crude oil volatility.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy.
prelimsPoints: RBI kept repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
Decision by Monetary Policy Committee (MPC).
Reason: persistent retail inflation, especially food prices.
'Withdrawal of accommodation' stance maintained.
RBI Governor: Shaktikanta Das.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its implications for India's economic growth and inflation trajectory.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in managing inflation while supporting economic recovery in the current global and domestic scenario.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance in controlling liquidity and achieving monetary policy objectives.
Examine the impact of global factors like crude oil prices and geopolitical tensions on India's monetary policy decisions.
GS3.imageDescription: RBI logo and a graph showing a stable interest rate line.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Test for Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology
GS3.explainer:
